__inputs.name: DS_PROMETHEUS
__inputs.label: Prometheus
__inputs.description: Prometheus data source scraping the OTLP -> Prometheus stream that carries mdk's OTel metrics. Provisioned automatically by infra/otel-collector/ (uid: prometheus).

description: mdk runtime overview: per-tenant + per-kind request rate, latency p50/p95/p99, and error rate over the OTel metrics mdk.jobs.completed and mdk.job.duration_ms. Source: src/movate/tracing/metrics.py. Prescriptive layer: triage flow at the top, per-chart triage notes, SLO threshold annotations matching infra/azure/modules/monitor-alerts.bicep, and drill-down links to Jaeger + Azure Monitor Workbooks. mdk does not export a per-route HTTP metric today; the 'route' axis from the brief degrades to 'kind' (job kind).

options.content:
## mdk - runtime overview

**What this shows.** Per-tenant runtime view: throughput (by status / by kind), latency p50/p95/p99, and error rate scoped to the selected `$tenant`. Source: `mdk.jobs.completed`, `mdk.job.duration_ms`.

**Normal range.**

- p95 < 2s, p99 < 5s under steady load (red threshold on the latency panel = the **`*-high-latency-p95`** SLO at 30s).
- Error rate < 1% per tenant (red threshold = the **`*-high-error-rate`** SLO at 10%).
- Throughput proportional to expected workload; flat-zero on a previously-active tenant is the **`*-availability-no-traffic`** signal.

**Triage flow.**

```mermaid
graph TD
  A[Tenant complaint or alert] --> B[Pick the tenant in the dropdown]
  B --> C{Throughput dropping?}
  C -- yes --> C1[Cross-check API health / availability alert]
  C -- no  --> D{Latency p95 rising?}
  D -- yes --> D1[Slice by kind on the p95-by-kind panel]
  D1 --> D2[Jaeger: filter service=movate-runtime tag tenant_id=$tenant]
  D -- no  --> E{Error rate up?}
  E -- yes --> E1[Drill into status=non-success in trace search]
  E -- no  --> F[Likely upstream UX issue; verify with Langfuse session view]
```

List fallback if Mermaid does not render:
1. Tenant selected -> throughput dropping? -> availability alert.
2. Latency p95 up -> slice by kind -> Jaeger tenant filter.
3. Error rate up -> trace search filtered on non-success.
4. Otherwise probe Langfuse for UX.

**Cross-surface.** KQL equivalent in `infra/azure-monitor/workbooks/tenant-ops.workbook.json`.

panels.description: Job completion rate (jobs/sec) split by terminal status, filtered to the selected tenant. From mdk.jobs.completed (status = success / error / safety_blocked / dead_letter).
panels.title: Throughput by status (selected tenant)

panels.description: Throughput split by job 'kind' attribute on mdk.jobs.completed for the selected tenant.
panels.title: Throughput by kind (selected tenant)

options.content:
**What this measures.** Tenant-scoped job completion rate split by `status` and by `kind`. Source: `mdk.jobs.completed`.

**Normal.** Throughput tracks tenant-expected workload. Flat-zero on a previously-active tenant is the **`*-availability-no-traffic`** signal (fleet-wide today; per-tenant variant deferred).

**If you see X.**

- `flat-zero for an active tenant` -> open Container Apps logs for the API; check tenant-level rate limit; verify the tenant's recent deploys.
- `large `status=error` slice` -> drill via the error-rate panel below + Jaeger filter `tenant_id=$tenant error=true`.
- `a single `kind` dominating unexpectedly` -> agent loop or scheduler misconfig; correlate with `mdk dev` agent inventory.

**ADR refs.** ADR 019 / ADR 031. **Alert rules.** `*-availability-no-traffic` (fleet).

panels.description: Latency percentiles over the mdk.job.duration_ms histogram (claim -> terminal status). Note: mdk.job.duration_ms attributes are {kind, status} only -- not 'tenant' -- so this panel is fleet-wide; the tenant variable does not filter latency.
panels.title: Latency p50 / p95 / p99 (fleet; red line = high-latency-p95 SLO @ 30s)

panels.description: p95 latency split by job kind from mdk.job.duration_ms (kind attribute).
panels.title: p95 by kind (red line = high-latency-p95 SLO @ 30s)

options.content:
**What this measures.** Wall-clock job duration percentiles (fleet-wide and split by `kind`). NB: `mdk.job.duration_ms` is **not** keyed on tenant; the `$tenant` selector does not filter these panels.

**Normal.** p95 < 2s, p99 < 5s. Red threshold line = **30000ms** (the **`*-high-latency-p95`** SLO).

**If you see X.**

- `p95 > 5s on a single kind` -> that kind is the hot path; in Jaeger, filter by `service=movate-runtime operation=agent.execute` plus the `kind` attribute and inspect `agent.turn`, `skill.<name>`, and `kb_search` children.
- `p95 > 30s` -> SLO alert active. Treat as SEV-2; bisect against the most recent deploy.
- `p99 spike alone` -> tail-only; provider hiccup. Inspect `provider` attribute on `agent.execute`.

**ADR refs.** ADR 013 / ADR 015 / ADR 024 (workflow/agent spans). **Alert rule.** `*-high-latency-p95`.

panels.description: Error ratio = non-success terminal jobs / all terminal jobs over the window, scoped to the selected tenant. Dead-letters are the status=dead_letter slice.

links.title: Jaeger - failed agent.execute for this tenant

panels.title: Error rate + dead-letter rate (selected tenant; red line = high-error-rate SLO @ 10%)

options.content:
**What this measures.** Per-tenant failure ratio (`status != success` and the dead-letter slice). Source: `mdk.jobs.completed`.

**Normal.** < 1%. Red line = **10%** (the **`*-high-error-rate`** SLO). Dead-letter line should sit at zero (the **`*-deadletter-spike`** alert fires on any non-zero count).

**If you see X.**

- `error rate > 1%` -> open Jaeger with `tenant_id=$tenant error=true`; group by `provider` first, then by `agent`.
- `error rate > 10%` (SLO line) -> SEV-1; check Activity Log for deploy correlation; if `503`-heavy, suspect runtime cold start (cross-link to the queue-and-pool dashboard for in-flight saturation).
- `dead-letter rate > 0` -> failed-poison retry budget exhausted; replay from Langfuse run trace + verify the storage `dead_letter` table state.

**ADR refs.** ADR 015 / ADR 019. **Alert rules.** `*-high-error-rate`, `*-deadletter-spike`.

title: mdk - runtime overview (throughput / latency / errors)
